violations.text: Walk-in cooler #4 was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. All food in unit (milk and yogurt) was observed at 44 DF. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All food in unit was moved to properly functioning refrigeration at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025

The door to the outside, located in the receiving area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

Walk-in gasket #4 was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. Walk-in cooler #8 was observed not working and in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025
The grout throughout the kitchen area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025

(Chemical) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025
Walk-in cooler #4 door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025

The following was in poor repair: 1. Peeling paint (above steam kettles) 2. Gaps in ceiling (walk-in cooler #5) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025

The ceiling in the walk-in cooler #5 area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025

The floor and wall junctures in the following areas do not have coving. 1. Equipment storage 2. Walk-In Cooler #7 The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the back food preparation area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (mac and cheese) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

The door to the outside, located in the receiving area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

Whipped cream was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024

Several chemicals throughout kitchen were stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024

The door to the outside, located in the receiving area is not tight-fitting. Hole was observed in kitchen behind tray line. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

Inspector observed walk-in cooler to be dripping water onto covered containers. PIC changed covers and informed about not storing anything under leak. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
Walk-in cooler was not in good repair, observed dripping water. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024
At the time of inspection, ambient of walk-in cooler was 47 degrees Fahrenheit. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in freezer has an accumulation of frost buildup. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024
The plumbing throughout the kitchen is in poor repair. 1. Dish machine (leak from top) 2. Steam kettle hose New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
The condensate from the walk-in cooler is draining on the floor, observed pooled liquid. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
